title: Lithocarpus apoensis
taxonomy: Lithocarpus apoensis is a species of plant in the family Fagaceae. It is classified as a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. The species is further classified as part of the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of trees commonly known as stone oaks. Lithocarpus apoensis is closely related to other species within the genus, such as Lithocarpus elegans and Lithocarpus griffithii. The plant's classification is based on its characteristics, including its leaf shape and fruit structure.
uses: Lithocarpus apoensis is known for its various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial in treating various health conditions. It is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, Lithocarpus apoensis has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Lithocarpus apoensis for medicinal purposes.

intro: Lithocarpus apoensis, also known as Apo oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus apoensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as tea and infusions, as well as being used in folk medicine to make decoctions and tonics.
